metadata.subject: Arts Education
metadata.description: Students engage in ensemble dance performance, working with established repertoire while developing collaborative rehearsal practices and ensemble discipline. This course emphasizes the importance of individual contribution to collective artistic expression and builds performance skills within a company context. Students experience the professionalism, commitment, and creative collaboration required in contemporary dance companies.
metadata.general_description: Develop as a performing dancer within an ensemble context, learning repertoire and collaborative rehearsal processes.

big_ideas.statement: Individual dancers contribute to collective artistic expression within a company or ensemble context.
big_ideas.elaboration: Each performer's technical skill, focus, and artistic commitment directly impact the overall quality and meaning of ensemble work; dancers must balance individual expression with ensemble unity.

big_ideas.statement: Collaborative rehearsal processes develop discipline, communication, and shared artistic understanding.
big_ideas.elaboration: Through structured rehearsal practices, dancers learn to interpret choreographic intent, provide and receive feedback, and work toward shared performance goals.

big_ideas.statement: Learning established repertoire develops performance mastery and builds connection to broader dance traditions.
big_ideas.elaboration: Studying and performing choreographed works by established choreographers provides dancers with models of artistic excellence and expands their understanding of diverse dance styles.

big_ideas.statement: Professional performance preparation and stage presence are essential aspects of company membership.
big_ideas.elaboration: Dancers develop their ability to execute choreography with technical precision, maintain focus under performance conditions, and engage audiences with authentic artistic expression.

competencies.raw: Learn and master choreographed repertoire with accuracy and artistic expression
i_can_statements: I can learn choreography efficiently and retain complex movement patterns and sequences
I can execute choreographed movement with technical precision and appropriate quality
I can interpret choreographic intent and express artistic meaning through performance
I can adapt my movement to match the style and aesthetic of various choreographers

competencies.raw: Develop ensemble unity and synchronized movement with fellow dancers
i_can_statements: I can maintain alignment, timing, and spatial relationships with ensemble members
I can anticipate and respond to the movement of other dancers in ensemble configurations
I can unify my movement quality and expression with the ensemble while maintaining clarity
I can adapt my performance to different ensemble formations and configurations

competencies.raw: Contribute creative ideas and energy to collaborative rehearsal processes
i_can_statements: I can bring focus, energy, and commitment to rehearsals and creative processes
I can share ideas and perspectives that contribute to ensemble understanding of choreography
I can engage in ensemble problem-solving to address technical or artistic challenges
I can support and encourage fellow company members in their artistic development

competencies.raw: Explore different interpretations and approaches to performing established choreography
i_can_statements: I can experiment with different dynamic qualities while maintaining choreographic accuracy
I can explore emotional depth and authenticity in performing repertoire
I can consider how personal background and experience inform my interpretation of choreography
I can work with directors and choreographers to refine my interpretation

competencies.raw: Analyze choreographic works and understand artistic intent
i_can_statements: I can identify the key themes, movement language, and artistic vision of choreographed works
I can trace the choreographer's use of composition techniques and their effect on meaning
I can understand the historical or cultural context of choreographed repertoire
I can articulate how choreographic choices communicate specific artistic intentions

competencies.raw: Evaluate my own performance and growth as an ensemble dancer
i_can_statements: I can assess my technical execution and identify areas for improvement in choreographed movement
I can reflect on my ensemble contribution and its impact on overall performance quality
I can recognize my personal strengths as a performer and areas for ongoing development
I can set goals for technical and artistic growth within a company context

competencies.raw: Understand the role of individual dancers within the collective artistic vision
i_can_statements: I can explain how my performance choices affect the ensemble and audience experience
I can recognize the interdependence of dancers in creating coherent ensemble works
I can reflect on the balance between individual expression and ensemble unity
I can appreciate the contribution of each company member to overall artistic success

competencies.raw: Develop professional standards and artistic discipline in company membership
i_can_statements: I can commit to rehearsal attendance, punctuality, and focused participation
I can maintain physical conditioning and injury prevention practices
I can respond constructively to feedback and direction from choreographers and company leadership
I can demonstrate the discipline and professionalism expected of company dancers

competencies.raw: Perform choreographed repertoire with technical excellence and artistic authenticity
i_can_statements: I can execute choreography with clarity, precision, and appropriate movement quality in performance settings
I can maintain focus, energy, and presence throughout performance of repertoire
I can express the emotional and artistic content of choreography through authentic performance
I can adapt my performance based on audience, venue, and performance conditions

competencies.raw: Collaborate effectively in rehearsal and performance environments
i_can_statements: I can communicate clearly with ensemble members and choreographers during rehearsal
I can provide and receive constructive feedback in supportive ways
I can coordinate with other dancers to maintain ensemble cohesion and unity
I can support the performance experience of fellow company members

competencies.raw: Document and share learning about choreographed works and company experiences
i_can_statements: I can record and review performances to assess technical execution and artistic interpretation
I can document insights about choreography, choreographers, and company experiences
I can share knowledge about repertoire and artistic processes with peers
I can articulate my learning and growth through written or verbal reflection

competencies.raw: Engage audiences through authentic and committed performance presence
i_can_statements: I can connect with audience members through genuine artistic expression and focus
I can respond to audience energy and adjust performance energy as appropriate
I can present myself professionally in performance and company contexts
I can speak about my experience as a company dancer and the significance of repertoire

competencies.raw: Connect ensemble dance experience to broader artistic and cultural contexts
i_can_statements: I can explore the historical and cultural significance of choreographed works and dance traditions
I can understand how ensemble performance relates to community, society, and cultural expression
I can make connections between dance repertoire and other art forms or disciplines
I can recognize the role of dance companies in contemporary arts ecosystems

competencies.raw: Learn from diverse choreographers and dance traditions represented in company repertoire
i_can_statements: I can study the distinctive styles and approaches of various choreographers
I can explore dance traditions from multiple cultures and time periods through performance
I can recognize how different choreographic approaches shape movement and meaning
I can develop appreciation for artistic diversity within ensemble dance

competencies.raw: Develop awareness of dance careers and professional pathways in performance
i_can_statements: I can explore career opportunities in professional dance companies and performance contexts
I can understand the training, preparation, and commitment required for professional dance
I can develop a performance portfolio that demonstrates my capabilities as a dancer
I can engage with mentorship and guidance from professional company members and choreographers

competencies.raw: Contribute to building a supportive and inclusive company culture
i_can_statements: I can model respect, support, and encouragement for fellow company members
I can contribute to creating an inclusive environment where all dancers feel valued
I can mentor younger or newer members of the company
I can participate in building a community committed to artistic excellence and mutual support

areas.name: Ensemble Performance and Choreographed Repertoire
topics: Learning and retaining choreographed movement patterns and sequences
Understanding choreographic intent and artistic style of various choreographers
Executing choreography with technical precision and artistic expression
Performing in various ensemble formations and configurations
Developing synchronization and unified movement quality across ensemble

areas.name: Rehearsal Processes and Collaborative Practices
topics: Structured rehearsal methodologies and efficient learning practices
Communication and feedback mechanisms in ensemble contexts
Problem-solving approaches for technical and artistic challenges
Collaborative creativity and shared artistic decision-making
Building ensemble cohesion and trust among company members
Supporting and encouraging fellow dancers in their development

areas.name: Performance Skills and Professionalism
topics: Stage presence, focus, and energy in performance contexts
Technical execution under performance conditions and pressure
Interpretation and emotional authenticity in performing repertoire
Preparation for performance and management of performance anxiety
Professional standards, attendance, punctuality, and commitment
Physical conditioning and injury prevention for sustainable performance

areas.name: Dance Company History, Traditions, and Artistic Vision
areas.raw: Dance Company History, Traditions, and Artistic Vision
topics: History and cultural significance of dance companies and repertoire
Different styles, traditions, and choreographic approaches represented in company works
The role of choreographers in shaping company identity and artistic direction
Community engagement and the role of dance companies in society
Professional expectations and company culture in ensemble contexts
Career pathways and opportunities in professional dance performance
